current: ring_test built + transition evaluation complete (INSIGHTS.md, transition_ratings.json)
notes: maps2/ is the second-gen world assembler; consumes tiles2/ (30px diamond, 16px face/level, first-class transitions). Worlds under maps2/worlds/<name>/.

First deliverable: worlds/ring_test — clear_water centre (spawn), 5 pizza slices (saturated_grass, lightdark_dirt, stone_mountain, black_mountain, regular_snow), elevation rising outward. Purpose: evaluate base-tile transitions.

Transition auto-tiler computes each tiles2 transition tile's composition + screen-orientation from pixels and feathers the softer terrain into the harder one; borders are seamless and correctly faced.

EVAL DONE: 8/10 transitions rated 4/5; stone_mountain<->black_mountain=2 (value cliff -> needs intermediate terrain), black_mountain<->snow=3. Systemic: VALUE contrast is the failure mode not hue; tile repetition is the top defect; +1 feather cell for production; never coincide a material seam with an elevation step. Full findings in maps2/worlds/ring_test/INSIGHTS.md + transition_ratings.json.
REFINED fill: pure ground is now ~75% clean base tiles / ~25% special accents (classified by off-material pixel fraction); cliff faces clean-only. Keeps flowers/mushrooms rare + special.

requests.text: Ring test-map evaluation done. Findings for you: (1) stone_mountain<->black_mountain and black_mountain<->snow are the weak transitions — both are VALUE cliffs (near-white/near-black); consider a mid-grey scorched-rock / ash bridge type or higher-contrast-aware transition tiles, and tint intermediate cells between BOTH neighbours (they currently go muddy purplish-grey belonging to neither). (2) Biggest quality lever across ALL pairs: more per-motif variety — puddle/scree/boulder/snow-blob motifs visibly repeat and tile along long borders; 2-3 more variants per transition motif + placement jitter would lift most 4s to 5s. Hue clashes (orange dirt/grey stone, orange/cyan) all absorbed fine — value is the only real problem.
